channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Over 77,000 customers' personal information is exposed in Fidelity Investments data breach
item.description: Fidelity Investments, one of the world's biggest asset managers, experienced a data breach in August, the company's second in 12 months.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Fake Jennifer Aniston bikini body ad
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Connect in a crisis with iOS 18's new video call feature for emergency services
item.description: Apple’s new Emergency SOS Live Video feature allows users to share live video footage of the emergency directly with first responders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DoT driving IT modernization to meet enterprise, local needs
item.description: Cordell Schachter, the Transportation Department CIO, said the agency’s DevSecOps capabilities are maturing as part of its digital transformation effort. The post DoT driving IT modernization to meet enterprise, local needs first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: A haunting Patch Tuesday for October: 117 updates (and 5 zero-day flaws)
item.description: This month’s Patch Tuesday delivers a large set of patches from Microsoft that fix 117 flaws, including five zero-day vulnerabilities (CVE-2024-43573, CVE-2024-6197, CVE-2024-20659, CVE-2024-43572 and CVE-2024-43583).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How Google used influence, ultimatum to cut controversial deal on California news
item.description: Google insisted on taxpayer money as part of the deal with California to help set a precedent for potential talks about payments to the news industry in other states, according to the people familiar with the talks.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Musk unveils long-awaited robotaxi
item.description: Tesla CEO Elon Musk unveiled the electric vehicle (EV) company’s long-awaited robotaxi Thursday night, showing off a fleet of sleek self-driving cars with no steering wheels or pedals.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Climate change gave significant boost to Milton's destructive rain, winds, scientists say
item.description: Scientists say human-caused climate change intensified deadly Hurricane Milton’s rainfall by 20 to 30% and strengthened its winds by about 10%.
item.dc:creator: Alexa St. John, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lesser-known xargs command is a versatile time saver
item.description: The xargs command may be one that many Linux users don’t know, but it’s also one that can save a lot of time and trouble on the command line. It’s an unusual command with some very handy uses.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Elon Musk unveils Tesla's 'Cybercab' as stock sinks
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Tesla unveiled its long-awaited robotaxi at a Hollywood studio Thursday night, though fans of the electric vehicle maker will have to wait until at least 2026 before they are available. CEO Elon Musk pulled up to a stage at the Warner Bros.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER and ALEX VEIGA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pentagon releases final CMMC rule, paving way for implementation
item.description: The Pentagon has taken another big step forward to make CMMC a reality. DoD projects the requirements will start showing up in contracts by mid-2025. The post Pentagon releases final CMMC rule, paving way for implementation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Las entidades locales tendrán un espacio de datos para ordenar el tráfico, reducir la tarifa energética y planificar mejor la gestión urbana
item.description: “Un paso decisivo e innovador hacia la digitalización y modernización de nuestras ciudades que contribuya a mejorar los servicios públicos que se prestan a la ciudadanía”.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Nube, ‘edge’ e IA: cómo sacar el máximo partido a este trinomio tecnológico
item.description: El mundo de TI está siendo testigo de una convergencia cada vez más cercana de la nube, el edge y la inteligencia artificial.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: A whopping 342,817 Lego Technic pieces are used to build a sports car
item.description: Lego and McLaren, the British supercar manufacturer, teamed up to create a fully drivable, life-sized replica of the McLaren P1 hypercar.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 18 Android settings that’ll strengthen your security
item.description: You might not know it from all the panic-inducing headlines out there, but Android is actually packed with practical and powerful security options.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: AI tracker charts growth in deepfakes ahead of election
item.description: The German Marshall Fund has launched a tool to track deepfakes created by artificial intelligence (AI) that are targeting elections and circulating in the U.S. and across the globe. The tool comes in a historic year for elections.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Amazon’s new AI-powered spotlight tech aims to help delivery drivers find packages faster
item.description: Amazon, in its quest for greater efficiency, has developed new systems to shave seconds off each package delivery and to help customers make faster buying choices, even for new product types that they may know little about.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Meta AI launches in Brazil, U.K., and more markets in a bid to beat ChatGPT
item.description: Meta Platforms said on Wednesday its artificial intelligence chatbot, Meta AI, will be available in 21 new markets, as it looks to compete with OpenAI’s ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Twin boys born conjoined celebrate 1st birthday after separation surgery
item.description: Twin brothers who were born conjoined recently celebrated their first birthday after undergoing successful separation surgery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Effective EDR: Balancing testing rigor and velocity
item.description: Various industries are still dealing with the consequences of the July 2024 technology outage which led to a "blue screen of death" and extreme disruptions. The post Effective EDR: Balancing testing rigor and velocity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: How to use your iPhone to get help by satellite
item.description: Apple’s decision to put satellite connections in iPhones is already saving lives, and as Hurricane Milton threatens Florida, now is the time for every US iPhone user to learn how the system works and why they should upgrade to iOS 18.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: A new way to ensure government and industry have enough cybersecurity people
item.description: "We could view cybersecurity as an entry level problem, but it is probably more of an intermediate and advanced role problem," Keith Clement said. The post A new way to ensure government and industry have enough cybersecurity people first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: US considers asking court to break up Google as it weighs remedies in the antitrust case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to break up Google after its ubiquitous search engine was declared an illegal monopoly, but it is just one of many possible remedies under review, according to a court filing.
item.dc:creator: SHAWN CHEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As global AI energy usage mounts, Nvidia claims efficiency gains of up to 100,000X
item.description: As concerns over AI energy consumption ratchet up, chip maker Nvidia is defending what it calls a steadfast commitment to sustainability.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Aryaka brings CASB into unified SASE fold
item.description: Aryaka continues to build out its SASE platform, adding new capabilities aimed at enhancing security and simplifying the adoption process for enterprises that want to consolidate their network and security deployments. At the heart of this update is Aryaka’s new cloud access security broker (CASB).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: California’s vetoed AI bill: Bullet dodged, but not for long
item.description: Artificial intelligence has the power to revolutionize industries, drive economic growth, and improve our quality of life. But like any powerful, widely available technology, AI also poses significant risks.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Oracle Introduces "Tip and Tail" Model for Java Library Development
item.description: Oracle touts a new release model for software libraries, aiming to balance innovation with stability in Java development
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: DoD wringing out zero trust concepts under assessment process
item.description: The Pentagon is also working with major cloud service providers like Microsoft and Google to evaluate their zero trust implementations. The post DoD wringing out zero trust concepts under assessment process first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: The Titan's former lead engineer says he felt pressured to get the submersible ready
item.description: The lead engineer for an experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreck of the Titanic testified Monday that he felt pressured to get the vessel ready to dive and refused to pilot it for a journey several years earlier.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and LISA J. ADAMS WAGNER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Advocacy groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending Swiss criminal probe of woman's death
item.description: Advocacy groups behind a so-called suicide capsule said Sunday they have suspended the process of taking applications to use it — which numbered over 370 last month — as a criminal investigation into its first use in Switzerland is completed.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, ABC 4
